title: Dog Travel Safety Laws by State — What You Need to Know
seoTitle: Dog Car Travel Laws by State – Is It Illegal? (2026)
metaDescription: Is it illegal to drive with an unrestrained dog? 6 states have laws. See which states require dog restraints and what fines apply. Complete 2026 guide.
author: GetPawsy Pet Care Team

content.text: Is It Illegal to Drive With an Unrestrained Dog?

There's no federal law requiring pet restraints in vehicles. However, 6 states have specific laws, and many others can cite distracted driving violations if an unrestrained pet causes an accident. Beyond legality, an unrestrained 60-lb dog in a 35 mph crash becomes a 2,700-lb projectile — a lethal threat to both the dog and passengers.
States With Specific Pet Restraint Laws

Hawaii: Dogs must be restrained; unrestrained pets in the front seat is a traffic violation ($97+ fine). Rhode Island: Proposed law requiring restraints (bill in committee). New Jersey: Unrestrained animals can be cited under animal cruelty laws ($250–$1,000 fine). Connecticut: Dogs cannot ride in the back of open trucks. Maine: Dogs in pickup beds must be restrained. Massachusetts: Proposed restraint bill (pending).

Even in states without specific pet laws, an unrestrained dog that causes an accident can result in distracted driving citations. Insurance companies may deny claims if an unrestrained pet contributed to the crash. AAA reports that 65% of dog owners engage in at least one distracting activity while driving with their pet.

Legal or not, the physics don't change. A 10-lb dog at 50 mph generates 500 lbs of projectile force. A 60-lb dog: 2,700 lbs. Crash-tested restraints — car seats for small dogs and harnesses for large dogs — protect both your pet and your family. It's the same logic as child car seats.

Regardless of state laws, restrain your dog with a proper car seat or crash-tested harness. Browse our [dog car travel safety](/dog/dog-car-travel-safety) collection for CPS-informed options. For small dogs, see our [booster seat vs car hammock comparison](/guides/dog-booster-seat-vs-car-hammock).

faq.question: Can I get a ticket for driving with my dog on my lap?
faq.answer: In Hawaii and New Jersey, yes — specific fines apply. In all other states, you can be cited for distracted driving if the dog causes unsafe behavior. It's also extremely dangerous in a crash.

faq.question: Do dogs need to be in the back seat?
faq.answer: It's strongly recommended. Front-seat airbag deployment can seriously injure or kill a dog. The back seat with a proper restraint is the safest position.

faq.question: What's the safest way to travel with a dog in a car?
faq.answer: A crash-tested harness clipped to the seatbelt (large dogs) or a secured booster seat (small dogs under 25 lbs). Both should be used in the back seat.
